ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a colleague's dbt mapping proposal.

You receive the full source/target context and EVERY proposed dbt model with its column notes. Review the proposal as a whole — cross-table consistency (shared keys, duplicated logic, conflicting casts), per-table correctness against the target schema, and PostgreSQL validity.

Verify that output columns use double-quoted aliases matching the target spec exactly (mixed-case names must not be left unquoted).

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise

Only flag real problems. Do not request stylistic rewrites.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 23, 4, 27, 785934, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

Columns:

- `name` text NULL
- `erp_number__c` text NULL
- `customer_tier__c` text NULL
- `region__c` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL
- `website` text NULL
- `billingcity` text NULL
- `billingcountry` text NULL
- `legacy_customer_id__c` text NULL

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact  (244 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `firstname` text NULL
- `lastname` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `phone` text NULL
- `title` text NULL
- `role__c` text NULL
- `preferred_language__c` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.installed_asset__c  (480 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `serial_number__c` text NULL
- `warranty_end_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `project__c` text NULL

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.opportunity  (303 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `stagename` text NULL
- `closedate` text NULL
- `amount` text NULL
- `currencyisocode` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.project__c  (190 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `project_status__c` text NULL
- `go_live_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `opportunity__c` text NULL

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
  CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
  CASE WHEN TRIM(name) = '' OR name IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(name)) END AS "Name",
  CAST(erp_number__c AS TEXT) AS "ERP_Number__c",
  CASE LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c))
    WHEN 'gold' THEN 'Gold'
    WHEN 'silver' THEN 'Silver'
    WHEN 'bronze' THEN 'Bronze'
    WHEN 'platinum' THEN 'Platinum'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
  CAST(LOWER(TRIM(region__c)) AS TEXT) AS "Region__c",
  CAST(INITCAP(TRIM(industry)) AS TEXT) AS "Industry",
  CAST(TRIM(website) AS TEXT) AS "Website",
  CAST(INITCAP(TRIM(billingcity)) AS TEXT) AS "BillingCity",
  CAST(INITCAP(TRIM(billingcountry)) AS TEXT) AS "BillingCountry",
  CAST(legacy_customer_id__c AS TEXT) AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
  CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
  NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
  0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- id: Direct map from source id; cast to text to satisfy target type.
  - Name <- name: Use INITCAP + TRIM for consistent casing; replace empty/NULL with 'Unknown' to satisfy NOT NULL constraint.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_number__c: Direct map from source erp_number__c, cast to text.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- customer_tier__c: Case-insensitive match against enum domain (Gold/Silver/Bronze/Platinum); NULL for unrecognized values.
  - Region__c <- region__c: Lowercased for consistency via LOWER + TRIM.
  - Industry <- industry: Normalized with INITCAP + TRIM for consistent casing.
  - Website <- website: Trimmed whitespace; cast to text.
  - BillingCity <- billingcity: INITCAP normalization applied; trim whitespace.
  - BillingCountry <- billingcountry: INITCAP normalization applied; trim whitespace.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- legacy_customer_id__c: Direct map from source legacy_customer_id__c to populate the legacy identifier column.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source date available; default to CURRENT_DATE::TEXT.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source date available; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Boolean flag not present in source; default 0 (not deleted).
Reasoning: The Account model directly maps source columns to the target schema with appropriate normalizations: INITCAP for human-readable fields like Name, BillingCity, BillingCountry, and Industry; LOWER + TRIM for Region__c; case-insensitive enum mapping for Customer_Tier__c against the (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) domain. Missing date/dedup columns receive sensible defaults (CreatedDate = CURRENT_DATE, LastModifiedDate = NULL, IsDeleted = 0).

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    c.id AS "Id",
    INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.firstname), 100)) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.lastname), 100)), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    UPPER(TRIM(c.email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(c.phone) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.title), 100)) AS "Title",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(c.role__c))
        WHEN 'decision maker' THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN 'end user' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN 'technical contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN 'executive sponsor' THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    UPPER(LEFT(TRIM(c.preferred_language__c), 2)) AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    c.id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c
JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a
    ON COALESCE(c.accountid, '') = COALESCE(a.legacy_customer_id__c, '')
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- contact.id: Source contact primary key, passed through as the Salesforce-style Contact ID.
  - FirstName <- contact.firstname: Trimmed, INITCAP'd, and limited to 100 chars.
  - LastName <- contact.lastname: Trimmed, INITCAP'd, defaulted to 'Unknown' if NULL. Required field per target spec.
  - Email <- contact.email: TRIM'd and uppercased for consistency.
  - Phone <- contact.phone: Trimmed whitespace only.
  - Title <- contact.title: INITCAP'd, limited to 100 chars.
  - Role__c <- contact.role__c: Mapped to enum domain (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor) with NULL fallback.
  - Preferred_Language__c <- contact.preferred_language__c: Uppercased first 2 chars for ISO language code normalization.
  - AccountId <- account.id, contact.accountid, account.legacy_customer_id__c: Joined contact.accountid to account.legacy_customer_id__c to resolve the proper Salesforce Account Id (001-prefixed). All models apply this same join for key consistency.
  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- contact.id: Source natural key preserved for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) to prevent type inference errors during materialization. Source data has no date values.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) to prevent type inference errors during materialization. Source data has no date values.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Constant 0 indicating no deleted rows in source.
Reasoning: Fixed two reviewer issues: (1) Added explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) for CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate to prevent PostgreSQL type inference errors during materialization. (2) Resolved the cross-table key inconsistency by joining contact to account on legacy_customer_id__c, so AccountId now references the proper Salesforce-style Account Id (with 001 prefix), matching the transform applied in all other models for join consistency.

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
  -- Asset Id: pass through source id directly (no Account prefix transformation)
  id AS "Id",
  
  -- Name: trim and use default if null (NOT NULL target column)
  COALESCE(TRIM(name), 'Unknown Asset') AS "Name",
  
  -- Serial number - direct mapping
  serial_number__c AS "Serial_Number__c",
  
  -- Warranty end date: parse multiple formats to ISO YYYY-MM-DD consistently
  CASE 
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c IS NULL THEN NULL
    -- Already ISO format or compatible with standard TO_DATE
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(warranty_end_date__c AS TEXT)
    -- US format MM/DD/YYYY
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' 
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
    -- EU format DD.MM.YYYY  
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$'
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
    -- Compact YYYYMMDD using TO_DATE (consistent with other tables)
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' 
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
  
  -- Account reference: pass through directly (consistent with Contact.AccountId and Opportunity.AccountId)
  account__c AS "Account__c",
  
  -- Project reference: pass through directly for consistency
  project__c AS "Project__c",
  
  -- Legacy_Asset_ID__: preserve source natural key for verification
  id AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
  
  -- Dates not available in source - explicit type cast per reviewer feedback
  CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
  CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
  
  -- IsDeleted: default to 0 (not deleted) since source doesn't track this
  0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'installed_asset__c') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- id: Pass through source id directly; corrected from incorrect Account-prefix ('001' || ...) transform.
  - Name <- name: TRIM and COALESCE to 'Unknown Asset' for NOT NULL constraint.
  - Serial_Number__c <- serial_number__c: Direct mapping from source column.
  - Warranty_End_Date__c <- warranty_end_date__c: Parse MM/DD/YYYY, DD.MM.YYYY, ISO, and YYYYMMDD formats via TO_DATE; consistent compact-date handling using TO_DATE instead of string concatenation.
  - Account__c <- account__c: Pass through directly (consistent with Contact.AccountId and Opportunity.AccountId), removing the incorrect conditional prefix transform.
  - Project__c <- project__c: Pass through directly for consistency with other cross-table key handling.
  - Legacy_Asset_ID__c <- id: Preserve source natural key for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not available in source; explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) per reviewer feedback instead of bare NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not available in source; explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) per reviewer feedback instead of bare NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default to 0 (not deleted) since source does not track deletion status.
Reasoning: Fixed four reviewer issues: (1) Id now passes through source id directly instead of incorrectly applying Account prefix logic; (2) Account__c and Project__c are passed through directly for cross-table consistency with Contact/Opportunity models; (3) YYYYMMDD date format now uses TO_DATE(col, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT instead of string concatenation; (4) CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate use explicit CAST(NULL AS TEXT) per PostgreSQL best practices.

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_stage AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
            WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stage_name_cleaned,
        CASE
            WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '12' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '31' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS close_date_cleaned
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')) AS "Name",
    COALESCE(stage_name_cleaned, 'Unknown') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(close_date_cleaned, CAST(NULL AS TEXT)) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            REGEXP_REPLACE(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(amount, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g'), '\.', ''),
                ',',
                '.'
            )::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, ''))) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 5, 3, 2162, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Installed_Asset__c`.

Previous SQL:
```sql

SELECT
  -- Asset Id: pass through source id directly (no Account prefix transformation)
  id AS "Id",
  
  -- Name: trim and use default if null (NOT NULL target column)
  COALESCE(TRIM(name), 'Unknown Asset') AS "Name",
  
  -- Serial number - direct mapping
  serial_number__c AS "Serial_Number__c",
  
  -- Warranty end date: parse multiple formats to ISO YYYY-MM-DD consistently
  CASE 
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c IS NULL THEN NULL
    -- Already ISO format or compatible with standard TO_DATE
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(warranty_end_date__c AS TEXT)
    -- US format MM/DD/YYYY
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' 
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
    -- EU format DD.MM.YYYY  
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$'
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
    -- Compact YYYYMMDD using TO_DATE (consistent with other tables)
    WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' 
      THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
  
  -- Account reference: pass through directly (consistent with Contact.AccountId and Opportunity.AccountId)
  account__c AS "Account__c",
  
  -- Project reference: pass through directly for consistency
  project__c AS "Project__c",
  
  -- Legacy_Asset_ID__: preserve source natural key for verification
```

```sql
  -- Account reference: resolve to Salesforce-style Account Id via legacy key join
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 6, 44, 719718, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Opportunity`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_stage AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
            WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stage_name_cleaned,
        CASE
            WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '12' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '31' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS close_date_cleaned
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')) AS "Name",
    COALESCE(stage_name_cleaned, 'Unknown') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(close_date_cleaned, CAST(NULL AS TEXT)) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            REGEXP_REPLACE(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(amount, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g'), '\.', ''),
                ',',
                '.'
            )::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, ''))) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_stage
```

Reviewer comments:
- `AccountId` is passed directly from source but must reference Salesforce-style Account IDs. Apply the same cross-table join/transform strategy used in Contact to resolve raw customer numbers to SFDC-style Account Ids.
- `StageName` fallback uses `'Unknown'`, which violates the declared enum domain `(Prospecting, Qualification, ..., Closed Lost)`. Replace with `NULL` to maintain a consistent NULL fallback policy for enum mismatches.

# Source database

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 6, 44, 719718, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Opportunity`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_stage AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
            WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stage_name_cleaned,
        CASE
            WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '12' AND
                 SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '31' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS close_date_cleaned
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')) AS "Name",
    COALESCE(stage_name_cleaned, 'Unknown') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(close_date_cleaned, CAST(NULL AS TEXT)) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            REGEXP_REPLACE(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(amount, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g'), '\.', ''),
                ',',
                '.'
            )::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, ''))) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_stage
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.

- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.

Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).

```sql
        WHEN LOWER(project_status__c) = 'in planning' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(project_status__c) = 'on hold' THEN 'On Hold'

    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(go_live_date__c, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a colleague's dbt mapping proposal.

You receive the full source/target context and EVERY proposed dbt model with its column notes. Review the proposal as a whole — cross-table consistency (shared keys, duplicated logic, conflicting casts), per-table correctness against the target schema, and PostgreSQL validity.

Verify that output columns use double-quoted aliases matching the target spec exactly (mixed-case names must not be left unquoted).

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise

Only flag real problems. Do not request stylistic rewrites.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.

- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).

- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 13, 44, 913921, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

```sql
WITH cleaned_stage AS (
    SELECT
        op.id,
        op.name,
        op.stagename,
        op.closedate,
        op.amount,
        op.currencyisocode,
        op.accountid AS raw_accountid,
        acc.id AS account_id_sfdc,
        CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(op.stagename, '')))
            WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stage_name_cleaned,
        CASE
            WHEN op.closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(op.closedate) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(op.closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(op.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(op.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' AND
                 SUBSTR(op.closedate, 5, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '12' AND
                 SUBSTR(op.closedate, 7, 2) BETWEEN '01' AND '31' THEN TO_DATE(op.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS close_date_cleaned
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} op
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} acc
        ON TRIM(op.accountid) = TRIM(acc.erp_number__c)
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')) AS "Name",
    stage_name_cleaned AS "StageName",
    close_date_cleaned AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            REGEXP_REPLACE(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(amount, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g'), '\.', ''),
                ',',
                '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, ''))) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    account_id_sfdc AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_stage
```

  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not available in source; set to NULL
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not available in source; set to NULL
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No source flag; defaults to 0

   This strips non-numeric except .,comma,-; removes all dots (thousand separators); swaps comma to dot. But wait - what if there's a negative sign? The regex keeps '-'. After removing dots and swapping comma to dot, we'd have something like "1234.56" or "-1234.56". ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 28, 52, 692282, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Contact`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    c.id AS "Id",
    INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.firstname), 100)) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.lastname), 100)), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    UPPER(TRIM(c.email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(c.phone) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(LEFT(TRIM(c.title), 100)) AS "Title",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(c.role__c))
        WHEN 'decision maker' THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN 'end user' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN 'technical contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN 'executive sponsor' THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    UPPER(LEFT(TRIM(c.preferred_language__c), 2)) AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    c.id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c
JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a
    ON COALESCE(c.accountid, '') = COALESCE(a.legacy_customer_id__c, '')
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:

```sql
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(op.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN op.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' AND

    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_stage
```

Three issues: (1) Amount regex bug — `REGEXP_REPLACE(..., '\.', '')` removes ALL dots before swapping commas. US-format amounts like '1234.56' become '123456', producing incorrect doubles. Must distinguish European dot+comma vs pure decimal formats. (2) Name uses `TRIM(COALESCE(name, ''))` — if name is NULL, result is empty string '', violating the NOT NULL constraint rule requiring 'meaningful defaults, not empty strings.' Use COALESCE(TRIM(name), 'Unknown'). (3) AccountId join uses `op.accountid = acc.erp_number__c` only, inconsistent with Contact/Installed_Asset__c (legacy_customer_id__c) and Project__c (OR fallback). Standardize the join pattern across all models.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 0, 39, 49, 277036, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Project__c`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    p.id AS "Id",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(COALESCE(p.name, '')) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(p.name))
    END AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(p.project_status__c) = 'active' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(p.project_status__c) = 'completed' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(p.project_status__c) = 'in planning' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(p.project_status__c) = 'on hold' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(p.project_status__c) = 'cancelled' THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c IS NOT NULL AND p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    o.id AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.id AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }} p
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a
    ON p.account__c = a.erp_number__c OR p.account__c = a.legacy_customer_id__c
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
    ON p.opportunity__c = o.id
```

Cross-table key inconsistency on Account__c: joins on `a.erp_number__c OR a.legacy_customer_id__c` (OR fallback), while Contact and Installed_Asset__c join only on legacy_customer_id__c.
